title: design: message_set_destroy guard mirrors message_get pattern consistently
description: message_set_destroy (lines 213-216 in methods/message.rs) replicates the empty-ids guard from message_get (lines 17-20) with identical error semantics. This is right because: (1) prevents accidental API misuse (caller forgets to check), (2) consistent pattern across the codebase, (3) guard fires before network call (no wasted round-trip), (4) Test 33 (lines 1309-1322) validates the guard behaves identically to message_get's. Both guard the same architectural decision: JMAP doesn't support 'fetch/destroy all', so pre-validation is mandatory.
close_reason: Design decision: good design, no action needed
